rag_rules: Fix AGREE diagnostics by resolving type errors in expressions and declarations on the same line as the error.
Move implementation-level bindings from component type annexes to implementation annexes when needed.
generic_rules: When AGREE reports type mismatches, ensure helper declarations match the types of their expressions.
Validate that all identifiers in AGREE contracts are visible in the current scope.
Prefer type-level guarantees over implementation-level ones unless necessary for binding.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE expressions.
Avoid package-qualified identifiers unless explicitly imported via `with` clauses.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new identifiers or types that are not present in the AADL model or its dependencies.
Do not place implementation-level references in component type annexes without proper scope alignment.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
AGREE validator diagnostics about binary expressions with <error> types
